judgment.summary: Profitable, fast-growing financial institution trading at a reasonable valuation with strong analyst support.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 17.9%; next earnings date is 2026-07-14.
positives: Revenue growth 15.9% YoY and earnings growth 56.1% YoY indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Quarterly earnings growth of 42.3% and analyst estimates for current Q (26.4%) and FY (43.6%) show continued positive momentum.
Operating margin at 34.1% and profit margin at 20.4% demonstrate robust profitability.
Forward P/E of 9.92x and PEG ratio of 0.69x suggest the stock is attractively valued relative to its growth.
Total cash of $1.05T and total debt of $815.58B indicate a manageable debt load with substantial liquidity.
22 analysts have a consensus 'buy' rating with a mean target of $146.84, suggesting significant upside potential.
negatives: Gross margin is 0.0%, which is unusual for a financial institution and may warrant further investigation into reporting methodology.
Return on equity at 7.6% and return on assets at 0.6% are modest.
Free cash flow (TTM) is not provided, making it difficult to assess cash generation beyond reported earnings.
Beta of 1.12 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Citigroup's stock price is influenced by broader market sentiment, with recent headlines highlighting fears of complacency in the junk debt market and sector-wide pressure affecting financial institutions. Despite these macroeconomic headwinds, Citigroup itself recently reported strong earnings, beating estimates significantly. The upcoming earnings date on July 14th will be a key catalyst to watch.

headlines.title: Junk Debt’s Red-Hot Rally Sparks Complacency Fears as Risk Rises
Phibro Animal Health Corporation (PAHC) Price Target Cut to $44 by Citi Amid Sector Pressure
